mappings.standard_remediation: Enforce AES-256 or AWS KMS encryption on the storage bucket to satisfy GDPR data-at-rest encryption mandates.

regulatory_links.explanation: Mandates the encryption of personal data at rest and in transit.
Requires baseline cybersecurity measures including cryptography.

mappings.standard_remediation: Remove wildcard (*) resource permissions and explicitly scope access to specific Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) to satisfy GDPR least-privilege processor obligations.

regulatory_links.explanation: Requires strict access control and least-privilege principles.

mappings.standard_remediation: Remove privileged security contexts and ensure containers do not run as the root user (runAsUser: 0) to comply with zero-trust mandates.

regulatory_links.explanation: Mandates strict access governance and privilege reduction at the container orchestration layer.

mappings.standard_remediation: Remove unverified AI telemetry or implement strict API authentication.

regulatory_links.explanation: Prohibits unverified data egress to shadow AI.
Third-party APIs must be vetted processors.
Ensures security of the software dependency tree.
